phrases.french: Les caves abritent une exposition d'art contemporain ?
phrases.english: The cellars host a contemporary art exhibition?
phrases.phonetic: lay KAV ah-BREET ewn eks-poh-zee-SYON dar kon-tom-poh-RAN
phrases.context: Pommery's cellars are remarkable — 18km of tunnels, 30 metres underground, with major contemporary art installations. A unique cultural as well as wine experience.

phrases.french: Qui a inventé le champagne brut ?
phrases.phonetic: kee ah an-von-TAY luh shom-PAH-nyuh BROOT
phrases.context: Madame Pommery invented the first commercially sold brut champagne in 1874. Before that, champagne was sweet. Most of the world now drinks brut style.

vocabulary.english: chalk (the rock of the Champagne caves)

tips: Pommery has the most spectacular cellars in Reims — 18km of tunnels with contemporary art installations
Madame Pommery invented brut champagne in 1874 — a fact worth mentioning during any tasting
The Victorian red-brick buildings above ground are also architecturally striking — modelled on Elizabethan style
